title: RAK4631 Renode platform is T-Echo-specific (wrong GPIO wiring + SPIM vs legacy SPI)
description: rak4631/support/rak4631.repl reuses nrf52840_lichen.repl, which hardcodes the T-Echo SX1262 wiring on gpio0 (CS=P0.24, RESET=P0.25, BUSY=P0.17, DIO1=P0.20) and models SPI1 as an easyDMA SPIM. The real RAK4631 (work/zephyr/boards/rak/rak4631/rak4631_nrf52840.dts) wires the SX1262 on gpio1 (CS=P1.10, RESET=P1.6, BUSY=P1.14, DIO1=P1.15) and uses compatible nordic,nrf-spi (legacy, non-DMA). Consequences in Renode: CS-deassert never reaches the SX1262 (state machine never resets), BUSY/DIO1 are never driven, and the easyDMA SPIM model does not match the legacy driver. RAK4631 is therefore currently non-functional in Renode despite being listed as supported. Give RAK4631 its own platform repl (or parameterize nrf52840_lichen.repl) with gpio1 wiring and the correct SPI controller model, then validate boot/SPI/TX like T-Echo (bead project-LICHEN-r7h4.5). Found during 3x codereview of the r7h4.5 fixes.
acceptance_criteria: A RAK4631 firmware boots in Renode with the SX1262 reachable (real opcodes) and LoRa TX reaching lichen-sim; 3 codereview passes.
notes: Grindable: correct wiring already documented in description (from rak4631_nrf52840.dts). Task is mechanical: fork nrf52840_lichen.repl, change gpio0→gpio1 pins, switch SPIM→legacy SPI model, validate boot+TX.
close_reason: Fixed: Created standalone RAK4631 Renode platform (rak4631.repl) with correct GPIO1 wiring (CS=P1.10, RESET=P1.6, BUSY=P1.14, DIO1=P1.15) and legacy SPI (no easyDMA). Platform now matches rak4631_nrf52840.dts instead of reusing T-Echo wiring from nrf52840_lichen.repl. Added README documenting GPIO differences.
